tool_input.summary: To evaluate whether Arabia Terra deposits reflect a single fluvial style or multiple depositional systems, GEO-node holdings most directly support (1) topographic/geomorphic mapping (MOLA regional DEMs; HRSC higher-resolution DTMs/orthoimages) and (2) compositional/context mapping (CRISM derived/targeted products; TES thermal inertia). Below are probed, citable PDS datasets/collections you can use for that analysis (note: meter-scale image mapping typically requires CTX/HiRISE from the PDS Imaging node, not GEO).

candidates.title: MARS EXPRESS HRSC DATA
candidates.reasoning: HRSC DTMs (derived topography) enable mapping slopes, basin geometry, channel long profiles, fan/delta surfaces and stratigraphic relationships needed to distinguish depositional systems (e.g., distributary fans vs deltaic foresets vs valley-fill).

candidates.title: MARS EXPRESS HRSC DATA
candidates.reasoning: Map-projected HRSC reflectance mosaics/orthos provide regional geomorphic context to classify deposit morphologies across Arabia Terra and compare multiple fluvial styles spatially.

candidates.title: MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER PEDR AND EGDR ARCHIVES
candidates.reasoning: MOLA MEGDR gridded topography is the standard regional DEM used for drainage-basin delineation, valley-network gradients, and broad depositional setting (uplands vs basins) across Arabia Terra.

candidates.title: MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER IEGDR ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: MOLA IEGDR provides additional gridded products useful for quantitative regional analyses (e.g., alternate grids/derived products), complementing MEGDR in assessing whether deposits occupy multiple geomorphic/topographic settings.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM TRDR Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: CRISM targeted RDR (TRDR) hyperspectral observations support mineralogic characterization (clays/sulfates/silica) of individual deposits; compositional differences can indicate different depositional environments (e.g., lacustrine deltaic vs fluvial/alluvial vs altered bedrock).

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM DDR Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: CRISM DDR provides geometry/ancillary derived parameters (photometry/atmosphere/pointing-related) used to correctly project and interpret CRISM spectra over deposits when comparing sites and depositional-system candidates.

candidates.title: MARS GLOBAL SURVEYOR TES-DERIVED THERMAL INERTIA
candidates.reasoning: TES-derived thermal inertia maps help differentiate indurated/rocky vs fine-grained sedimentary surfaces and can support interpretations of depositional facies variability across Arabia Terra deposits.

candidates.title: CRISM ATO S-Band LMLP Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Optional CRISM special product: map-projected LML-processed images can be useful for broad spectral-parameter comparisons across regions, complementing standard TRDR/DDR when screening for compositional diversity among deposits.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Geosciences node at https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/.

Directory layout:
  mission/ → dataset_or_bundle/ → volume/ (PDS3) or sub-collections (PDS4)

Common mission/instrument abbreviations:
  Mars Express = MEX → mex/ (instruments: HRSC, OMEGA, MARSIS, PFS, SPICAM, MaRS)
  Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter = MRO → mro/ (instruments: HiRISE, CTX, CRISM, SHARAD, MCS)
  Mars Science Laboratory / Curiosity = MSL → msl/ (instruments: ChemCam, APXS, CheMin, SAM, Mastcam, MAHLI, DAN)
  Mars 2020 / Perseverance = M2020 → m2020/ (instruments: PIXL, SHERLOC, Mastcam-Z, SuperCam, RIMFAX)
  Mars Exploration Rovers (Spirit=MER2, Opportunity=MER1) → mer/ (instruments: Pancam, Mini-TES/MTES, APXS, MB, MI)
  Mars Global Surveyor = MGS → mgs/ (instruments: MOC, MOLA, TES, MAG)
  Mars Odyssey = ODY → ody/ (instruments: THEMIS, GRS, NS)
  Phoenix = PHX → phx/ (instruments: TEGA, MECA, SSI, OM, RAC)
  Viking = VL1/VL2/VO1/VO2 → viking/ (instruments: camera, IRTM, MAWD)
  MESSENGER = MESS → messenger/ (instruments: MDIS, GRNS, XRS, MLA, MASCS)
  Magellan = MGN → mgn/ (instruments: SAR, altimetry, radiometry, emissivity)
  LRO → lro/ (instruments: LOLA, Diviner, LROC, Mini-RF, LAMP)
  GRAIL → grail/ (instruments: LGRS)
  NEAR → near/ (instruments: NLR, MSI, XGRS, MAG)

PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

Extracting dataset_id from labels:
  - PDS3: fields.VOLUME.DATA_SET_ID (or top-level DATA_SET_ID)
  - PDS4 bundle: fields.Identification_Area.logical_identifier
  - PDS4 collection: fields.Identification_Area.logical_identifier

YOUR TOOLS (4 tools):
  1. pds_geo_list_missions() — returns all 24 mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP. Use this to identify which mission to explore.
  2. pds_geo_list_dataset_dirs(path) — lists sub-directory names under a mission (e.g. path='mex/'). Cheap HTTP, no label parsing. Each dir gets a pds_hint ('PDS3'/'PDS4'/null) from its naming convention. Use this to see what datasets exist, then pick which to probe.
  3. pds_geo_probe_datasets(paths) — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Recurses one level to find leaf nodes. Returns dataset_id, title, pds_version, and slimmed fields. Accepts a list of paths (max 20) so you can batch multiple probes in one call.
  4. pds_geo_inspect_collections(path) — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection*.xml labels. Returns collection-level logical_identifiers. Use ONLY after probe_datasets confirms a PDS4 bundle exists.

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires you to return dataset candidates. Queries come from published scientific papers and always imply specific PDS datasets. NEVER dismiss a query as 'interpretation' or 'conceptual'. Always return at least one candidate.

  2. Prefer CALIBRATED and REDUCED data products over raw/EDR when the query implies scientific analysis. Scientists use calibrated (RDR, CDR) or derived (DDR) products. Look for directory names containing 'calibrated', 'rdr', 'cdr', 'ddr'.

  3. For PDS4 datasets, use inspect_collections ONLY for the top 2-3 most relevant bundles. Return the COLLECTION-level LID when a :data or :calibrated collection exists, not just the bundle LID.

  4. When both PDS3 and PDS4 versions exist, prefer the PDS4 collection-level identifier.

WORKFLOW (follow this order):
  Step 1: Infer the mission(s) and instrument(s) from the query using the abbreviation table above.
  Step 2: Call list_dataset_dirs for the relevant mission directory (e.g. 'mex/'). Scan the directory names and pds_hints to identify the most promising dataset directories.
  Step 3: Call probe_datasets with the most relevant paths (batch them — up to 20 paths in one call). This returns dataset_id, title, and pds_version for each.
  Step 4: If any probed datasets are PDS4 bundles and you need collection-level LIDs, call inspect_collections on the top 2-3.
  Step 5: Return the candidates with dataset_id, title, and reasoning.

  Skip list_missions if you already know the mission directory from the abbreviation table. Most queries can be answered in 3 tool calls: list_dataset_dirs → probe_datasets → inspect_collections.

Constraints:
  - Stay under 8 tool calls per query. This is a hard limit.
  - Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
  - You MUST return at least one candidate for every query.
Do the Arabia Terra deposits record only one fluvial style or a diverse range of depositional systems?

parts.args: {"summary":"To evaluate whether Arabia Terra deposits reflect a single fluvial style or multiple depositional systems, GEO-node holdings most directly support (1) topographic/geomorphic mapping (MOLA regional DEMs; HRSC higher-resolution DTMs/orthoimages) and (2) compositional/context mapping (CRISM derived/targeted products; TES thermal inertia). Below are probed, citable PDS datasets/collections you can use for that analysis (note: meter-scale image mapping typically requires CTX/HiRISE from the PDS Imaging node, not GEO).","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-DTM-V1.0","path":"mex/mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1/mexhrs_2001/","title":"MARS EXPRESS HRSC DATA","mission":"MEX","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"HRSC DTMs (derived topography) enable mapping slopes, basin geometry, channel long profiles, fan/delta surfaces and stratigraphic relationships needed to distinguish depositional systems (e.g., distributary fans vs deltaic foresets vs valley-fill)."},{"dataset_id":"MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-MAPPROJECTED-V3.0","path":"mex/mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-mapprojected-v3/mexhrs_1003/","title":"MARS EXPRESS HRSC DATA","mission":"MEX","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"Map-projected HRSC reflectance mosaics/orthos provide regional geomorphic context to classify deposit morphologies across Arabia Terra and compare multiple fluvial styles spatially."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-MEGDR-L3-V1.0","path":"mgs/mgs-m-mola-5-megdr-l3-v1/mgsl_300x/","title":"MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER PEDR AND EGDR ARCHIVES","mission":"MGS","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"MOLA MEGDR gridded topography is the standard regional DEM used for drainage-basin delineation, valley-network gradients, and broad depositional setting (uplands vs basins) across Arabia Terra."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-MOLA-5-IEGDR-L3-V2.0","path":"mgs/mgs-m-mola-5-iegdr-l3-v2/mgsl_2xxx/","title":"MGS MARS ORBITER LASER ALTIMETER IEGDR ARCHIVE","mission":"MGS","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"MOLA IEGDR provides additional gridded products useful for quantitative regional analyses (e.g., alternate grids/derived products), complementing MEGDR in assessing whether deposits occupy multiple geomorphic/topographic settings."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mro_crism_trdr_2101:data_trdr","path":"mro/mro-m-crism-3-rdr-targeted-v1/mrocr_2101/","title":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM TRDR Data Collection","mission":"MRO","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"CRISM targeted RDR (TRDR) hyperspectral observations support mineralogic characterization (clays/sulfates/silica) of individual deposits; compositional differences can indicate different depositional environments (e.g., lacustrine deltaic vs fluvial/alluvial vs altered bedrock)."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mro_crism_ddr_1001:data_ddr","path":"mro/mro-m-crism-6-ddr-v1/mrocr_1001/","title":"Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM DDR Data Collection","mission":"MRO","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"CRISM DDR provides geometry/ancillary derived parameters (photometry/atmosphere/pointing-related) used to correctly project and interpret CRISM spectra over deposits when comparing sites and depositional-system candidates."},{"dataset_id":"MGS-M-TES-5-TIMAP-V1.0","path":"mgs/mgs-m-tes-5-timap-v1/mgst_9001/","title":"MARS GLOBAL SURVEYOR TES-DERIVED THERMAL INERTIA","mission":"MGS","pds_version":"PDS3","reasoning":"TES-derived thermal inertia maps help differentiate indurated/rocky vs fine-grained sedimentary surfaces and can support interpretations of depositional facies variability across Arabia Terra deposits."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:crism_ato_s_iof_lml:data_lmlp","path":"mro/urn-nasa-pds-crism_ato_s_iof_lml/","title":"CRISM ATO S-Band LMLP Data Collection","mission":"MRO","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Optional CRISM special product: map-projected LML-processed images can be useful for broad spectral-parameter comparisons across regions, complementing standard TRDR/DDR when screening for compositional diversity among deposits."}]}
